A plain unstyled document

This is a long paragraph of ordinary body text with no author layout whatsoever: no flex, no grid, no positioning, and no explicit widths. It is just words, and they should wrap at a comfortable readable measure of roughly seventy-three columns rather than stretching edge to edge across a very wide browser window like this one, exactly as a real browser's default body would.

A second paragraph continues the same plain prose so that we can observe where the right edge of the text column falls and confirm the readable measure keeps the line length comfortable instead of running the full window width.
